name: Taipei Open
stakes: This week offers India's shuttlers a crucial opportunity to consolidate form and gain valuable ranking points ahead of bigger tournaments later in the season.
summary:
India's badminton contingent arrives in Taipei for the Super 300 event, looking to build momentum after a mixed bag of recent results. All eyes will be on PV Sindhu, who recently clinched the Japan Open title with a scorching win over Akane Yamaguchi, signaling a strong return to form. However, her subsequent Round of 16 exit at the China Open, where she frittered away four match points against Chen Yufei, highlights the need for consistency.

In men's singles, Lakshya Sen and HS Prannoy will be keen to make deep runs. While no specific draw details are out yet, both will be aiming to find their rhythm and challenge the top contenders. India's formidable men's doubles pair, Satwik & Chirag, ranked fourth in the world, lead the charge in their discipline, always a threat for silverware. The young women's doubles duo of Treesa & Gayatri, alongside the top mixed doubles pair of Dhruv Kapila & Tanisha Crasto, will also be looking to make their mark on the international stage.

indiaContingent.name: HS Prannoy
indiaContingent.note: Veteran aiming for a deep run
